instructor.name: Vivek Sarin
instructor.office_hours: MW 3:30-4:30pm, via Zoom https://tamu.zoom.us/j/99726975773; or by appointment

chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content: Parallel Computing. (3-0). Credit 3. Overview of parallel computing technology and programming methods; includes multiprocessor architectures, programming tools, parallel performance, parallel algorithms, and applications of parallel computing. Prerequisite: Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331; junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in CSCE 315 or CSCE 331; junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
The main objective of this course is to provide an opportunity for our undergraduate students to learn about parallel computing technology and to develop programming skills on traditional and emerging platforms for parallel computation. Upon successful completion of the course, a student:
* will be able to design parallel algorithms and develop software implementations on parallel computers;
* will be able to analyze the performance of parallel implementations on various parallel platforms; and
* will know the architecture of modern parallel computing systems.
* will engage in independent scholarship and research to develop solutions of computational problems and to analyze and evaluate the approaches developed as part of course project

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Optional
None
Notes:
Instructor will provide lecture videos, copies of lecture slides, and links to resources on the internet on the course web site on Canvas.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignments and projects will be available on the course web site on Canvas and submissions will have to be uploaded to Canvas.
Course grade will be assigned on the basis of quizzes (40%), assignments (30%), a minor project (10%), and a major project (20%). The following scale will be used:
A=90-100%, B=80-89%, C=70-79%, D=60-69%, F=0-59%. Scores are rounded up to the nearest integer.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content: Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Late assignments and projects are accepted with 10% penalty for each day late up to a maximum of 3 days (e.g., a score of 80 will be reduced to 72 when submitted within 24 hours after the deadline; it will be reduced to 64 when submitted between 24 and 48 hours after the deadline).
Late quizzes will not be accepted.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Tentative Schedule
| Week | Topics (tentative) |
|---|---|
| 1 | Orientation, Introduction to parallel computing |
| 2 | Parallel algorithm design |
| 3, 4, 5 | Programming for shared-memory |
| 6, 7 | Parallel architectures |
| 8 | Parallel algorithms I – Low level kernels; Minor project due |
| 9 | Programming for distributed-memory |
| 10 | Parallel algorithms II – Sorting |
| 11, 12, 13 | Emerging parallel platforms - GPUs |
| 14 | Parallel algorithms III - Graph algorithms |
| 15 | Other computing paradigms (e.g., Map-Reduce); Major project due |
